content.cn_text: 旁白：第三部分。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Section 3.

content.cn_text: 你将听到一位名叫乔尔的学生向他的导师谈论他的研究以及
content.en_text: You will hear a student, called Joel, talking to his tutor about his research and the

content.cn_text: 农民对农业新发展的态度。
content.en_text: farmer's attitudes towards new developments in agriculture.

content.cn_text: 首先，您有时间查看第5页上的问题21到26。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 26 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题21到26。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：欧文博士。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Doctor Owen.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：进来吧，乔尔。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Come in Joel.

content.cn_text: 好的，你的研究进展如何？
content.en_text: Right, so how's your research going?

content.cn_text: 你是不是在研究农民对农业新技术的看法？
content.en_text: You're looking at how farmers feel about new technology and agriculture aren't you?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：嗯，实际上比那更广泛。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Well, actually it's more general than that.

content.cn_text: 我正在研究他们对农业各种新发展的态度。
content.en_text: I'm looking at their attitudes to a variety of new developments in agriculture.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：好的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Okay.

content.cn_text: 你的项目有标题了吗？
content.en_text: Have you got a title for the project yet?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：我正在考虑一个简单的标题，比如《农民对采用新耕作方式的态度》。
content.en_text: [Joel]: I was thinking of something simple, like farmers attitudes towards the adoption of new farming practices.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：那可能有点太笼统了。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: That's possibly a bit too general.

content.cn_text: 我认为标题需要更具体一些。
content.en_text: I think the title needs to be a bit more specific.

content.cn_text: 你会研究哪种类型的农业？
content.en_text: What kind of farming will you be looking at?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：嗯，实际上会因情况而异。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Well, that'll vary, actually.

content.cn_text: 但我希望与我样本中的十位农民交谈。
content.en_text: But I want to speak to each of the ten farmers in my sample.

content.cn_text: 所以我选择了都在同一个地区的农场。
content.en_text: So I've chosen farms which are all in the same region.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：好的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Okay.

content.cn_text: 嗯，那么我会具体说明它们的位置。
content.en_text: Well, I'd specify where they are then.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：好的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Right.

content.cn_text: 我会修改标题来做到这一点。
content.en_text: I'll amend the title to do that.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：好的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Okay.

content.cn_text: 你决定进行面对面访谈有什么特别的原因吗？
content.en_text: Is there any particular reason why you've decided to do face-to-face interviews?

content.cn_text: 这会相当耗时，不是吗？
content.en_text: It'll be quite time-consuming, won't it?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：是的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Yes.

content.cn_text: 因此我确实考虑过进行电话访谈。
content.en_text: And I did consider doing telephone interviews for that reason.

content.cn_text: 尤其是我没有必要亲自去看他们工作的地方。
content.en_text: Especially as it's unnecessary for me to actually see the places where they work.

content.cn_text: 只是根据我的经验，很多农民不喜欢打电话。
content.en_text: It's just that, in my experience, a lot of farmers don't like talking on the phone.

content.cn_text: 所以如果我亲自去见他们，他们会更愿意自由交谈。
content.en_text: So they'll be more likely to talk freely if I see them in person.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：是的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Yes.

content.cn_text: 你可能说得对。
content.en_text: You're probably right.

content.cn_text: 很好。
content.en_text: Good.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：那么，我想调查的第一件事是农民究竟是如何了解到农业新发展的
content.en_text: [Joel]: Well, then the first thing I want to investigate is how the farmers actually find out about new developments in agriculture

content.cn_text: 他们可能想要采纳的。
content.en_text: that they might want to adopt.

content.cn_text: 他们使用什么来源。
content.en_text: What sources they use.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：好的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Right.

content.cn_text: 那你打算怎么做呢？
content.en_text: So how are you going to do this?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：嗯，我可以在事前为他们制作一份信息来源清单，
content.en_text: [Joel]: Well, I could make a checklist of sources of information for them to look at in advance,

content.cn_text: 这样他们就可以勾选出他们实际使用的。
content.en_text: so they could tick off the ones they actually use.

content.cn_text: 比如农业杂志、农产品广告、电视和互联网等等。
content.en_text: Things like farming magazines, farm product advertisements, television and the internet and so on.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：直接问他们而不是给他们看清单可能更有用。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: It might be more useful to ask them rather than show them a list.

content.cn_text: 对一两个开放性问题的自发回答可能会给你更准确的情况。
content.en_text: A spontaneous response to one or two open questions might give you a more accurate picture.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：好的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: All right.

content.cn_text: 那我就这么做。
content.en_text: I'll do that then.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：现在，跟我说说你还想涵盖的其他一些问题。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Now, talk me through some of the other issues you want to cover.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：嗯，还有政府与农民沟通的方式。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Well, there's the way the government communicates with farmers.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：好的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Right.

content.cn_text: 嗯，其中一件事是，农民抱怨政府向所有农民发送相同的信息，
content.en_text: Well, one thing is, farmers complain that the government sends out the same information to all of them,

content.cn_text: 无论他们是养禽户、奶农还是耕地农民。
content.en_text: whether they're poultry farmers or dairy farmers or arable farmers.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：所以他们收到所有这些信息，但其中一半实际上与他们无关。
content.en_text: [Joel]: So they get all this information, but half of it doesn't actually apply to them.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：没错。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Exactly.

content.cn_text: 所以你可以研究一下这一点。
content.en_text: So you could look at that.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：好的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Right.

content.cn_text: 然后我想研究英国农民对在农场进行任何改变所需成本的态度。
content.en_text: Then I wanted to look at attitudes of British farmers to the cost of making any changes on their farms.

content.cn_text: 根据我读过的文章，英国农民原则上不反对投资新机械和新实践，
content.en_text: According to articles I've read, farmers in Britain aren't in principle against investing money in new machinery and new practices,

content.cn_text: 但他们实际上只是太忙了，没时间计算财务影响。
content.en_text: but they're actually just too busy to work out what the financial implications are.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：是的，而且他们很多人似乎也没有考虑向他们的会计师寻求财务建议。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Yes, and a lot of them don't seem to consider getting financial advice from their accountants about it either.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：好的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Right.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：我一直在澳大利亚进行一些关于绵羊饲养实践的研究。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: I've been some research in Australia on sheep rearing practices.

content.cn_text: 如果你愿意，我可以给你参考文献。
content.en_text: I can give you the reference, if you like.

content.cn_text: 基本上，似乎大多数澳大利亚绵羊养殖户愿意在仅有少量研究的基础上冒险采用新实践。
content.en_text: Basically, it appears that the majority of Australian sheep farmers are willing to risk adopting new practices on the basis of just a few pieces of research.

content.cn_text: 他们通常不会等到支持这些改变的证据确凿无疑才行动。
content.en_text: They don't usually wait until the evidence in favour of the changes is overwhelming.

content.cn_text: 他们很乐意在此之前行动，以便抢占先机。
content.en_text: They're quite happy to act before then so they can get ahead of the game.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：好的，我会看看。
content.en_text: [Joel]: OK, I'll take a look at that.

content.cn_text: 谢谢。
content.en_text: Thanks.

content.cn_text: 旁白：在您听讨论的其余部分之前，您有时间查看第6页上的问题27到30。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 27 to 30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听并回答问题27到30。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 27 to 30.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：我们说过也要看看我一直在审阅的一些阅读材料。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: We said we'd also look at some of the reading material I've been reviewing.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：是的，我又看了一遍《当代农业手册》，它显然是最新的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Yes, I had another look at contemporary farming manual, which is clearly bang up to date.

content.cn_text: 它涵盖了农民可能想知道的很多东西，但它太枯燥了。
content.en_text: It covers a lot of things a farmer might want to know, but it's so dull.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：哦，完全同意。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Oh, absolutely.

content.cn_text: 我发现它读起来真的很费劲。
content.en_text: I found it really hard going.

content.cn_text: 我无法想象有哪个农民会读完它。
content.en_text: And I can't imagine any farmer waiting through it.

content.cn_text: 甚至没有像样的图片。
content.en_text: There aren't even any decent pictures.

content.cn_text: 你有时间看《小农场经营》吗？
content.en_text: Did you have time to look at running a small farm?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：是的，虽然我只读了一部分，因为它虽然有趣，但不怎么学术。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Yes, although I only read bits of it because despite being entertaining, it wasn't very academic.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：我知道。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: I know.

content.cn_text: 而且很多信息不是误导就是完全错误。
content.en_text: And a lot of the information is either misleading or simply wrong.

content.cn_text: 我真惊讶它竟然出版了。
content.en_text: I'm surprised it got published at all.

content.cn_text: 我想农业出版界不会给它很高的评价。
content.en_text: I don't imagine the farming press will rate it very highly.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：您推荐了《农业与经济学》。
content.en_text: [Joel]: You recommend agriculture and economics.

content.cn_text: 我本以为它会非常难。
content.en_text: I expected that it would be really difficult.

content.cn_text: 标题有点让人望而却步，但我认为它非常出色，所以我买了它。
content.en_text: The titles are a bit off-putting, but I thought it was brilliant, so I've bought it.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：是的。
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Yes.

content.cn_text: 有些人认为它过于依赖世界特定地区的农业实践，
content.en_text: Some people think it relies too heavily on farming practices in specific parts of the world,

content.cn_text: 但支撑这些的理论是普遍适用的。
content.en_text: but the theories that underpin these are universal.

content.cn_text: 这就是为什么它是所有农业学习者的必读教材。
content.en_text: And that's why it's a required textbook for anyone studying agriculture.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：好的。
content.en_text: [Joel]: Right.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：你还遇到了什么？
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: What else did you come across?

content.cn_text: 乔尔：我从图书馆借了《如何在农业中生存》。
content.en_text: [Joel]: I borrowed how to survive in farming from the library.

content.cn_text: 我以为它会是一个有趣的背景资料，而且我喜欢它的非正式风格，
content.en_text: I thought it would be interesting background, and I liked the informal style,

content.cn_text: 但结果它是在很多年前写的。
content.en_text: but it turned out to have been written years ago.

content.cn_text: 欧文博士：是的，和所有其他事物一样，农业潮流已经改变了，
content.en_text: [Dr. Owen]: Yes, and like everything else, farming fashions have changed,

content.cn_text: 所以它迫切需要一个新版本。
content.en_text: so it's spardly in need of a new addition.

content.cn_text: 乔尔：我能问你一个关于……的问题吗？
content.en_text: [Joel]: Can I ask you a question about...

content.cn_text: 旁白：第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: That is the end of section 3.

content.cn_text: 您现在有半分钟时间检查您的答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在翻到第7页的第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4 on page 7.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21-26.

items.title: What does Dr Owen advise Joel to include in the title of his project?
Why has Joel decided to do face-to-face interviews?

options.title: B
options.content: to limit the time he spends on the project

items.title: Joel agrees to investigate how farmers get information on new developments

options.title: A
options.content: by showing them a series of pictures.

options.title: C
options.content: by sending them a checklist in advance.

items.title: Concerning government communication with farmers, the speakers agree that

options.title: B
options.content: it is often insufficient for farmers' needs.

items.title: According to Joel's reading about the cost of making changes, many British farmers

options.title: A
options.content: leave investment decisions to their accountants.

options.title: B
options.content: have too little time to calculate the costs of new methods.

options.title: C
options.content: are reluctant to spend money on improvements.

items.title: A survey of Australian sheep farmers found that most of them

options.title: A
options.content: are usually reluctant to make changes.

options.title: B
options.content: make changes based on limited research.

options.title: C
options.content: want plenty of evidence before they make changes.

groups.desc: Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–G, next to questions 27–30.
groups.title: Questions 27-30.
